summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The Counter interface represents a named counter that tracks the progress of a map/reduce job.
- It has methods to set the display name of the counter, get the name and display name of the counter, get the current value of the counter, set the value of the counter, and increment the counter by a given value.

CORE LOGIC:
- The Counter interface is part of the Map-Reduce framework and is used to track the progress of a job.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The Counter interface does not have any specific inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The Counter interface does not have any specific dependencies.

This chunk does not contain any core logic.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

This chunk does not contain any inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:

This chunk depends on the CounterGroupBase class and the Counter class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

Holds per job/task counters, defined either by the Map-Reduce framework or applications.

CORE LOGIC:

Each Counter can be of any Enum type.
Counters are bunched into CounterGroups, each comprising of counters from a particular Enum class.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

Counters are bunched into CounterGroups.

DEPENDENCIES:

org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.GenericCounter
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.AbstractCounterGroup
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.CounterGroupBase

org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.AbstractCounters
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.CounterGroupFactory
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.FrameworkCounterGroup

package org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce;

import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience;
import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability;

/**
 * Used to describe the priority of the running job. 
 * DEFAULT : While submitting a job, if the user is not specifying priority,
 * YARN has the capability to pick the default priority as per its config.
 * Hence MapReduce can indicate such cases with this new enum.
 * UNDEFINED_PRIORITY : YARN supports priority as an integer. Hence other than
 * the five defined enums, YARN can consider other integers also. To generalize
 * such cases, this specific enum is used.
 */
@InterfaceAudience.Public
@InterfaceStability.Evolving
public enum JobPriority {
  VERY_HIGH,
  HIGH,
  NORMAL,
  LOW,
  VERY_LOW,
  DEFAULT,
  UNDEFINED_PRIORITY;
}


SUMMARY:

This code chunk defines an enumeration of job priorities for the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework. The JobPriority enum includes six priority levels: VERY_HIGH, HIGH, NORMAL, LOW, VERY_LOW, DEFAULT, and UNDEFINED_PRIORITY. The DEFAULT priority is used when

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The QueueState enum represents the state of a queue in a Hadoop MapReduce system.

CORE LOGIC:
- The enum has three possible values: STOPPED, RUNNING, and UNDEFINED.
- The enumMap is a map that maps state names to QueueState objects.
- The getState method takes a state name as input and returns the corresponding QueueState object. If the state name is not found in the enumMap, it returns UNDEFINED.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The QueueState enum does not take any inputs or produce any outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The QueueState enum does not depend on any other classes or packages.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The class provides a basic implementation of the Counter interface.

CORE LOGIC:
- The class implements the Counter interface and provides methods to set and get the name, display name, and value of the counter.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The class has methods to set and get the name, display name, and value of the counter.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The class does not depend on any other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The class provides a common implementation of the generic counter group in both mapred and mapreduce package.

CORE LOGIC:
- The class uses a ConcurrentMap to store the counters and provides methods to add, find, and iterate over the counters.
- It also provides methods to set and get the display name of the group.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The class provides methods to read and write the group data to/from a DataInput/DataOutput stream.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The class depends on the Counter and CounterGroupBase interfaces, as well as the Text, WritableUtils, and StringInterner classes from the Apache Hadoop framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The CounterGroupBase interface provides a common interface for counter groups in Apache Hadoop MapReduce.

CORE LOGIC:
- The interface includes methods for getting and setting the name and display name of the group, adding and finding counters, and incrementing all counters by a group of counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The interface includes methods for adding and finding counters, which take a name, display name, and value as input, and return the counter that was added or found.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The interface depends on the Counter interface and the Writable interface.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The CounterGroupFactory class is an abstract class that provides common implementation of the group factory in both mapred and mapreduce packages. It contains methods for creating new counter groups, mapping framework groups, and checking whether a group name is a framework group.

CORE LOGIC:

The CounterGroupFactory class contains methods for creating new counter groups, mapping framework groups, and checking whether a group name is a framework group. It also contains methods for creating new framework groups and getting the id of a framework group.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The CounterGroupFactory class takes in the name and displayName of a counter group, as well as a limits object that specifies the counters limits policy. It returns a new counter group based on the input parameters.

DEPENDENCIES:

The CounterGroupFactory class depends on the Counter, FileSystemCounter, JobCounter, and TaskCounter classes from the mapreduce package, as well as the ResourceBundles class from the util package. It also depends on the Limits class from the counters package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class provides a framework for implementing counter groups in both mapred and mapreduce packages. It includes methods for adding, finding, and incrementing counters, as well as reading and writing counter data.

CORE LOGIC:

The class includes a counter facade for framework counters, which allows for compatibility between old and new interfaces. It also includes methods for adding, finding, and incrementing counters, as well as reading and writing counter data.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class takes in a class object representing the counter enum class, and provides methods for adding, finding, and incrementing counters. It also includes methods for reading and writing counter data.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters package and the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.util package. It also depends on the org.apache.hadoop.io package for reading and writing counter data.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
GenericCounter is a class that extends AbstractCounter and implements the Counter interface. It provides a generic implementation of a counter with the ability to set and increment a value, as well as retrieve the name, display name, and value of the counter.

CORE LOGIC:
The GenericCounter class contains a constructor that takes in the name and display name of the counter, as well as a constructor that takes in the name, display name, and initial value of the counter. It also contains methods for setting and incrementing the value of the counter, as well as retrieving the name, display name, and value of the counter.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The GenericCounter class has methods for reading and writing the counter's name, display name, and value to and from a DataInput and DataOutput stream.

DEPENDENCIES:
The GenericCounter class extends the AbstractCounter class and implements the Counter interface, which provides methods for setting and incrementing the value of the counter, as well as retrieving the name, display name, and value of the counter. It also imports the necessary classes for reading and writing data to and from a stream.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Maintains the maximum of a sequence of long values.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

- The getReport method returns the string representation of the aggregator.
- The getCombinerOutput method returns an array of values as the outputs of the combiner.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

- The reset method does not take any input and simply resets the aggregator.
- The getReport method does not take any input and returns the string representation of the aggregator.
- The getCombinerOutput method does not take any input and returns an array of values as the outputs of the combiner.

DEPENDENCIES:
- This interface does not import any other classes.
- It is not a child or collaborator of any other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk is to implement the generic reducer of Aggregate.

CORE LOGIC:
The key algorithms or processing patterns visible in this chunk are:
- The setup method is called to set up the context for the reducer.
- The reduce method is called for each key-value pair in the input, where the key is a Text object indicating the type of aggregation to perform, and the values are the data to be aggregated.
- The reduce method extracts the type of aggregation from the key, creates a ValueAggregator object of the appropriate type, and adds each value to the aggregator.
- The reduce method then writes the aggregated value to the output using the original key.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The data flowing in and out of this chunk is:
- The input consists of key-value pairs, where the key is a Text object and the value is a Text object.
- The output consists of key-value pairs, where the key is the original key and the value is the aggregated value as a Text object.

DEPENDENCIES:
The key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk are:
- ValueAggregatorJobBase
- ValueAggregatorDescriptor
- UniqValueCount
- ValueAggregatorBaseDescriptor
- ValueAggregator
- Text
- Writable
- WritableComparable
- Reducer
- Context
- IOException
- InterruptedException

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The main purpose of this code is to implement a value aggregator that computes the histogram of a sequence of strings.

CORE LOGIC:
- The code uses a TreeMap to store the histogram data, where the keys are the string values and the values are the corresponding frequency counts.
- The addNextValue method adds the given value to the histogram, incrementing the frequency count for that value.
- The getReport method returns a string representation of the basic statistics of the histogram, including the number of unique values, minimum value, median value, maximum value, average value, and standard deviation.
- The getReportDetails method returns a string representation of the list of value/frequency pairs in the histogram.
- The getCombinerOutput method returns a list of value/frequency pairs in the histogram, which can be used by the reducer.
- The getReportItems method returns a TreeMap representation of the histogram data.
- The reset method resets the aggregator by creating a new empty TreeMap.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The addNextValue method takes a single string value as input and adds it to the histogram.
- The getReport method returns a string representation of the basic statistics of the histogram.
- The getReportDetails method returns a string representation of the list of value/frequency pairs in the histogram.
- The getCombinerOutput method returns a list of value/frequency pairs in the histogram.
- The getReportItems method returns a TreeMap representation of the histogram data.
- The reset method does not take any input and simply resets the aggregator by creating a new empty TreeMap.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The code does not import any specific classes or interfaces.
- The code does not depend on any parent or collaborator classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- DBRecordReader is a RecordReader that reads records from a SQL table.
- Emits LongWritables containing the record number as key and DBWritables as value.

CORE LOGIC:
- DBRecordReader uses a PreparedStatement to execute a SQL query and retrieve the results.
- It uses LIMIT and OFFSET clauses in the query to handle splits and retrieve only a portion of the data.
- It uses ReflectionUtils to create a new instance of the inputClass.

- It also depends on the results, pos, key, value, connection, statement, dbConf, split, and tableName.

CORE LOGIC: The interface requires implementations to provide methods for writing the object's fields to a PreparedStatement and reading the fields from a ResultSet.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The write method takes a PreparedStatement as an argument and sets the fields of the object in the statement. The readFields method takes a ResultSet as an argument and reads the fields of the object from the result set.
DEPENDENCIES: This interface does not depend on any other classes or interfaces.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

It uses data-driven WHERE clause splits to read records.

and then use them to generate the SELECT query.
